summary: iOS | Intermittent | DFU/Language Change fails at >90%
scenario: DFU or Language Change fails at >90% completion, causing firmware version to revert after restart or language to revert after brief use
description_clean: Intermittent DFU (Device Firmware Update) or Language Change fails at >90% completion. After a failed DFU, the device shows as up-to-date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After a failed Language Change, the device may briefly use the new language then restart and revert to the previous language. This issue is intermittent and does not occur every time.
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android
Connect to a Poly Bluetooth audio device
Select Firmware Update (DFU) or Language Change
Initiate the DFU or Language Change process
Wait for the update to progress beyond 90% completion
Observe the update process until it completes or fails
If DFU fails: Check device status in app (should show as up-to-date)
Power cycle the device (Press and hold Power button to turn off, then press again to turn on)
Reconnect to the device in Poly Studio Mobile app
Check firmware version in Device Settings
If Language Change fails: Observe device behavior after the update completes
Power cycle the device (Press and hold Power button to turn off, then press again to turn on)
Check which language is active on the device
expected: DFU: Firmware update completes successfully at 100%. Device shows updated firmware version after restart. Language Change: New language is applied and persists after device restart.
actual: DFU: Update fails at >90% completion. Device shows as up-to-date in the app but reverts to outdated firmware after power cycling. Language Change: Device briefly uses the new language then restarts and reverts to the previous language. Issue is intermittent.
